research_goal: Run a bounded read-only walkthrough of Clink UAT dashboard across login, analytics, transactions, balances, customers, products, developer, and settings surfaces. Stay inside Clink first-party pages, avoid destructive actions, and return partial findings if verification, loading, or external-domain blocks prevent progress.

products.name: Clink UAT Dashboard
products.notes: Read-only UAT walkthrough. Credentials are resolved from the local encrypted credential store or environment variables. Stay on uat-dashboard.clinkbill.com and related Clink first-party domains. Do not open GitHub, external documentation, payment-provider, support, or app-store links; only note their visible labels or URLs when relevant. If a page gets stuck or verification blocks progress, stop that scenario with a partial summary.

scenarios.title: Authenticate and inspect analytics overview
scenarios.persona: Product manager validating the dashboard entry experience
scenarios.goal: Reach the authenticated dashboard and understand the analytics overview first screen.
steps: Open the analytics URL
If redirected to login, sign in with the provided safe credentials
Wait for the authenticated dashboard to load
Inspect the top-level navigation, active page label, date controls, summary cards, charts, and empty states
Summarize first-screen clarity, blockers, and next best actions without trying to fix account or verification state
success_criteria: The test account reaches an authenticated page
The analytics overview or closest dashboard state is visible
Primary navigation and page purpose are understandable
No destructive action, payment, account mutation, export, or settings change is performed

scenarios.title: Inspect Data Insights and analytics controls
scenarios.goal: Assess whether Data Insights and analytics controls are discoverable and useful without changing data.
steps: Start from the authenticated dashboard or analytics page
Open the Data Insights navigation item or closest analytics section
Inspect available subpages, filters, date ranges, metric selectors, chart controls, and loading states
Use safe read-only interactions such as expanding menus or changing visible filters if they do not mutate account data
If a filter or chart stays loading, capture the state once and summarize discoverability, terminology, and missing guidance
success_criteria: Data Insights or analytics navigation can be found
Filters and chart controls are understandable
Read-only exploration does not trigger destructive actions
The agent can identify what a PM should inspect next

scenarios.title: Inspect Transactions read-only workflow
scenarios.persona: Product manager validating payment operations visibility
scenarios.goal: Understand whether transactions can be found, scanned, filtered, and inspected safely.
steps: Navigate to Transactions from the sidebar
Inspect table columns, search, filters, status labels, pagination, and empty states
Open a transaction detail only if it is clearly read-only, safe, and visible without additional verification
Do not refund, capture, resend, export, create, edit, or perform any account mutation
Summarize whether an operations user can understand transaction health and next actions
success_criteria: Transactions navigation is discoverable
The list or empty state is understandable
Read-only detail inspection is possible or clearly blocked
No destructive or irreversible action is performed

scenarios.title: Inspect balances, customers, and subscriptions read-only
scenarios.persona: Product manager evaluating business operations surfaces
scenarios.goal: Review balance, payout, customer, and subscription surfaces for clarity and safe read-only access.
steps: Navigate to Balances and inspect visible balance, payout, currency, and empty-state information
Navigate to Customers and inspect visible list, search, filters, and safe read-only detail affordances
Navigate to Subscriptions and inspect visible list, status, pricing, billing-cycle, or empty-state information
Avoid payout creation, customer edits, subscription changes, exports, or destructive actions
Summarize clarity, missing guidance, and cross-section consistency
success_criteria: Balances, Customers, and Subscriptions entries are discoverable
Each section communicates its current state clearly
Read-only information can be inspected without mutation
Potentially destructive controls are identified and avoided

scenarios.title: Inspect products, developer, and settings surfaces read-only
scenarios.persona: Product manager validating setup and administration discoverability
scenarios.goal: Review product catalog, developer integration, skills, and settings surfaces without changing configuration.
steps: Navigate to Products and inspect visible catalog/list structure, status, and safe read-only details
Navigate to Developers or Skills and inspect visible integration guidance, API/key warnings, documentation link labels, or setup copy without opening external links
Navigate to Settings and inspect available category labels or read-only overview panels without editing values
Avoid creating products, rotating keys, revealing full secrets, saving settings, changing account configuration, or opening external GitHub/docs links
Summarize setup clarity, trust warnings, and administration risks; stop with a partial summary if any page blocks progress
success_criteria: Products, developer, skills, and settings areas are discoverable
Setup or integration guidance is understandable
Secrets or sensitive keys are not revealed or copied
No configuration change is saved
